primary_sources.finding: Successful JSON verification separates the verified bundle from verificationResult; only signature.certificate and verifiedTimestamps are described as non-workflow-manipulable, while statement.predicate may be workflow-controlled.
A bundle contains verification material and signed content; X.509 identity extensions are consumed at verification time, and transparency or RFC3161 timestamps support signing-time validation.

finding: Policy must derive repository, signer-workflow, and issuer identity from the cryptographically verified certificate, not from statement predicate fields. Signed predicate metadata remains workflow-controlled and cannot independently establish trust.

verification.route: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files
cases: exact certificate identity, timestamp, subject digest, and predicate type accepted
matching workflow-controlled predicate cannot override a mismatched certificate repository

uncertainty: The evaluator supports known certificate field aliases but has not yet been exercised against a live gh attestation verify --format=json payload from this repository.
falsification_route: Capture a real GitHub CLI verification JSON payload, run it through the evaluator, and reject or revise the adapter if certificate fields do not normalize without manual rewriting.
trust_limit: This adapter does not perform cryptographic verification. It only consumes a verifier result and enforces a trust boundary over its fields.
